mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 60% of instances where the unemployment rate crosses the 0.3pp threshold within a quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, which triggers positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are flat or negative when industrial production growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month across multiple consecutive observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 240.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 240.0 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which supports wage growth and consumer confidence, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to predict positive returns in more than 50% of cases or shows negative rank correlation over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, which systematically produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 55% of quarters with positive GDP growth or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity and weakening business investment, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Markets generate positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances following two consecutive months of industrial production declines.
